description: Load the GroEL-GroES complex (1AON) into PyMOL. Create a visualization of the internal folding chamber: 1. Color the GroES heptamer (chains O, P, Q, R, S, T, U) yellow, the GroEL cis ring (chains A, B, C, D, E, F, G) green, and the GroEL trans ring (chains H, I, J, K, L, M, N) blue. 2. Create a surface representation of the complex. 3. Apply a clipping plane (slab) to vertically slice the complex and reveal the internal cavities. 4. Save a high-quality PNG figure to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/groel_chamber_sliced.png. 5. Measure the internal diameter of both rings using the C-alpha atoms of an apical domain residue (Residue 261) across diametrically opposed subunits. For the cis ring, measure between Chain A Residue 261 CA and Chain D Residue 261 CA. For the trans ring, measure between Chain H Residue 261 CA and Chain K Residue 261 CA. 6. Write a report to ~/PyMOL_Data/groel_report.txt containing: the 3-letter amino acid code of Residue 261, the measured cis ring diameter (in Å), the measured trans ring diameter (in Å), and the calculated expansion difference (cis minus trans).
